message:
fix(reactor): per-test fd namespace isolation and post-rearm cleanup assertion

Epoll reactor tests that manipulate high file descriptors now allocate
per-test fd ranges via an atomic counter (NEXT_FD_REUSE_TEST_MIN_FD)
that advances by FD_REUSE_TEST_FD_STRIDE (64) on each call to
next_fd_reuse_test_min_fd(). Previously all fd-reuse tests shared the
same FD_REUSE_TEST_MIN_FD constant (50,000), which caused sporadic
EBADF failures when parallel test threads dup'd fds into overlapping
ranges and then closed each other's descriptors.

Affected tests:
- deregister_delete_failure_preserves_bookkeeping_for_retry
- modify_closed_fd_cleans_stale_bookkeeping_for_fd_reuse
- reused_fd_cannot_register_under_new_token_until_stale_token_removed

IO driver: added an assert_with_log after the rearm test to verify the
driver's registration table is empty after dropping the registration,
catching leaked registrations that would affect subsequent tests sharing
the same IoDriverHandle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
